mechanics.text:
CHARACTER CREATION
Personal History Your character' = background story should be fairly easy to create as you already have the Life Path to build details based the paths and events the character  lived through, and add any other elements as you see fit. Make sure to account for traits purchased after the Life Path: If the char- acter has ay Contact Traits_ details about those contacts can form the basis of  interesting relationships for the character Likewise enemies can become the basis for ongoing plots_ creative as you can; your character'$ background determines who he or she really is
Character Personality Finally; you need personality for your character something that makes him or her distinct individual: If you don"t have one in mind you can use the Life Path to flesh out various personali- ty aspects_ How did your character respond to events in the Life Path? How did his schooling and careers (or Iack thereof) affect his outlook on life? Why does he have certain traits_ and now have other traits affected the way he relates t0 others? Use your imagination, and try t0 give your character at least one or two per- sonality quirks that make him Or her stand out. If you re having trouble with details try basing the character one from favorite book or movie_ But don't sweat it too much; roleplaying the character iS bound t0 draw out parts of the character' per- sona that yoU wouldn' have thought up otherwise
SAMPLE CHARACTERS Following this section are eight  sample characters New players can use them to start playing right away; without going through the character creation process Players can also base their own characters the samples; customizing them exchanging some of the listed skills and traits for ones of equal value (with the   Bamemaster' $ permission). Finally; the gamemaster can use these characters as major non player char- acters (see 2051. Each sample character entry includes a illustration of the character and briet story about his 0r her background: The story was created using the Life Path; with details fleshed out afterward who the character' $ Enemies are what exact Disability he she has_ exactly what kind of Interest the char- acter picked Up in college and The footnotes each story point to exactly what paths and events each character experienced and should serve as useful example of the char acter creation system in action For each character; filled-out character recora sheet appears on the facing page_ This sheet shows all the Attributes_ skills traits and equipment derived from the Life Path and the character' story: Players may photocopy and use these sheets games so that they can dive right in and start playing: Please note that some gamemaster intervention was used tO round out the histories of the sample characters; may not be possible to re-create all sample characters using the Character Creation rules_ Even so_ all of the sample characters provided are per- fectly lega for players t0 use in campaign_
MIBCELLANEOUS DETAILS Now you need only work out few more minor details_ Write everything out neatly on fresh sheet Of paper or on copy of the character record sheet (P_ 2171, and you are ready t0 play!
Name Come Up with name for your character; or else other char- acters will have to call him ney you_ In the BattleTech uni- verse , names are more less the same as they are today: Jou need inspiration baby-name book or the ever-popular phone-book trick: Grab your local phone book, flip through quickly and put your finger on random name_ If the first or Iast name looks good_ take it, then flip again for the other name; starting Clan character does not nave last name because he has (presumably) not yet earned Bloodname
Phyelcal Description What does your character look Iike? What does he or she wear? Keep mind that your character' affiliation can have major impact on his or her appearance: to get sense of each affiliation' look and feel, see the color pages_
